# diagramkit — Full Reference

> Diagram rendering CLI & library. Converts .mermaid, .excalidraw, and .drawio files to SVG/PNG/JPEG/WebP with automatic light/dark mode support and WCAG color contrast optimization.

## Installation

All rendering engines (mermaid, excalidraw, draw.io) are bundled:
  npm add diagramkit
  diagramkit warmup

For raster output — PNG, JPEG, WebP (optional, for targets like email or Confluence):
  npm add sharp

## CLI Reference

### Commands

  diagramkit render <file-or-dir> [options]    Render diagram file(s) to images
  diagramkit warmup                            Pre-install Playwright chromium browser
  diagramkit init                              Create a .diagramkitrc.json config file
  diagramkit install-skills [--global]         Copy diagramkit skills to .claude/skills/ (or ~/.claude/skills/ with --global)
  diagramkit --version                         Show version
  diagramkit --help                            Show help

### Render Options

  --format <svg|png|jpeg|webp>     Output format (default: svg)
  --theme <light|dark|both>        Theme variants to render (default: both)
  --scale <number>                 Scale factor for raster output (default: 2)
  --quality <number>               JPEG/WebP quality 1-100 (default: 90)
  --force                          Re-render all, ignore manifest cache
  --watch                          Watch for file changes and re-render
  --no-contrast                    Disable dark SVG contrast optimization
  --type <mermaid|excalidraw|drawio>  Filter to specific diagram type
  --output <dir>                   Custom output directory (default: .diagrams sibling)
  --output-dir <name>              Output folder name (default: .diagrams)
  --manifest-file <name>           Manifest filename (default: diagrams.manifest.json)
  --no-manifest                    Disable manifest tracking
  --same-folder                    Output in same folder as source files
  --dry-run                        Show what would be rendered without rendering
  --quiet                          Suppress informational output, only show errors
  --json                           Output results as JSON (for CI/scripting)

### Examples

  diagramkit render .                              Render all in cwd
  diagramkit render flow.mermaid                   Single file
  diagramkit render ./docs                         Default SVG output
  diagramkit render ./docs --format png --theme light --scale 2  Raster output for email/Confluence
  diagramkit render . --watch                      Watch mode
  diagramkit render . --format webp --quality 85   WebP output when raster is required
  diagramkit render . --type drawio                Only draw.io files
  diagramkit render . --theme dark --no-contrast   Raw dark output

### Format Guidance

  Prefer SVG by default for rendered diagrams.
  Use raster formats (PNG/JPEG/WebP) only when the destination explicitly needs them,
  especially email or Confluence embeds.

## JavaScript API

### Core Functions

  render(source: string, type: DiagramType, options?: RenderOptions): Promise<RenderResult>
    Render a diagram from source content string.

  renderFile(filePath: string, options?: RenderOptions): Promise<RenderResult>
    Render a diagram from a file path on disk. Detects type from extension.

  renderAll(options?: BatchOptions): Promise<RenderAllResult>
    Batch render all diagrams in a directory tree. Outputs go to .diagrams/ folders.
    Returns { rendered: string[], skipped: string[], failed: string[] }.

  watchDiagrams(options: WatchOptions): () => void
    Watch for diagram file changes and re-render. Returns cleanup function.

### Conversion

  convertSvg(svg: Buffer | string, options: ConvertOptions): Promise<Buffer>
    Convert SVG to raster format (PNG/JPEG/WebP) using sharp.
    sharp is dynamically imported — only required when called.

### Configuration

  loadConfig(overrides?: Partial<DiagramkitConfig>, dir?: string): DiagramkitConfig
    Load merged config: defaults -> global (~/.config/diagramkit/config.json)
    -> local (.diagramkitrc.json, walks up) -> overrides.

  getDefaultConfig(): DiagramkitConfig
    Returns default configuration values.

### Extension Mapping

  getExtensionMap(overrides?: Record<string, DiagramType>): Record<string, DiagramType>
    Get the full extension-to-type mapping, merged with optional overrides.

  getDiagramType(filename: string, map?): DiagramType | null
    Resolve diagram type from a filename. Tests longest extensions first.

  getMatchedExtension(filename: string, map?): string | null
    Get the matched extension string from a filename.

  getAllExtensions(map?): string[]
    Return all known extensions.

  getExtensionsForType(type: DiagramType, map?): string[]
    Return extensions that map to a given diagram type.

### Discovery

  findDiagramFiles(dir: string, config?): DiagramFile[]
    Recursively find all diagram source files. Skips hidden dirs and node_modules.

  filterByType(files: DiagramFile[], type: DiagramType, config?): DiagramFile[]
    Filter diagram files by resolved diagram type.

### Manifest

  hashFile(filePath: string): string
    SHA-256 content hash (first 16 hex chars), prefixed with "sha256:".

  isStale(file: DiagramFile, format?, config?, theme?, manifest?): boolean
    Check if a file has changed since last render.

  filterStaleFiles(files: DiagramFile[], force: boolean, format?, config?, theme?): DiagramFile[]
    Filter to only files that need re-rendering.

  updateManifest(files: DiagramFile[], format?, config?): void
    Update manifests after successful renders. Groups by directory.

  cleanOrphans(files: DiagramFile[], config?): void
    Remove orphaned outputs for deleted source files.

  readManifest(sourceDir: string, config?): Manifest
  writeManifest(sourceDir: string, manifest: Manifest, config?): void
  ensureDiagramsDir(sourceDir: string, config?): string
  getDiagramsDir(sourceDir: string, config?): string

### Color Utilities

  postProcessDarkSvg(svg: string): string
    Fix dark SVG contrast by darkening high-luminance fills while preserving hue.

  hexToRgb(hex: string): [number, number, number]
  rgbToHsl(r: number, g: number, b: number): [number, number, number]
  hslToHex(h: number, s: number, l: number): string
  relativeLuminance(r: number, g: number, b: number): number

### Browser Lifecycle

  warmup(): Promise<void>
    Pre-warm the browser pool (launches Chromium).

  dispose(): Promise<void>
    Explicitly dispose the browser pool and close Chromium.

## TypeScript Interfaces

### DiagramType
  type DiagramType = 'mermaid' | 'excalidraw' | 'drawio'

### OutputFormat
  type OutputFormat = 'svg' | 'png' | 'jpeg' | 'webp'

### Theme
  type Theme = 'light' | 'dark' | 'both'

### DiagramFile
  interface DiagramFile {
    path: string       // Absolute path to source file
    name: string       // Filename without extension
    dir: string        // Directory containing the file
    ext: string        // File extension (e.g. .mermaid, .mmd, .excalidraw, .drawio)
  }

### RenderOptions
  interface RenderOptions {
    format?: OutputFormat           // Default: 'svg'
    theme?: Theme                   // Default: 'both'
    quality?: number                // JPEG/WebP quality 1-100. Default: 90
    scale?: number                  // Scale for raster output. Default: 2
    contrastOptimize?: boolean      // Dark SVG contrast fix. Default: true
    mermaidDarkTheme?: Record<string, string>  // Custom dark theme variables
    config?: Partial<DiagramkitConfig>
  }

### RenderResult
  interface RenderResult {
    light?: Buffer     // Light theme output (SVG string as Buffer, or raster binary)
    dark?: Buffer      // Dark theme output
    format: OutputFormat
    width?: number     // SVG width in pixels (SVG output only)
    height?: number    // SVG height in pixels (SVG output only)
  }

### RenderAllResult
  interface RenderAllResult {
    rendered: string[]   // Files that were rendered
    skipped: string[]    // Files skipped (unchanged, manifest cache hit)
    failed: string[]     // Files that failed to render
  }

### BatchOptions
  interface BatchOptions extends RenderOptions {
    dir?: string           // Root directory to scan. Default: cwd()
    force?: boolean        // Force re-render, ignoring manifest. Default: false
    type?: DiagramType     // Filter to specific diagram type
  }

### WatchOptions
  interface WatchOptions {
    dir: string                              // Root directory to watch
    onChange?: (file: string) => void         // Callback after render
    renderOptions?: RenderOptions
    config?: Partial<DiagramkitConfig>
  }

### ConvertOptions
  interface ConvertOptions {
    format: 'png' | 'jpeg' | 'webp'
    density?: number       // Scale multiplier for SVG rasterization. Default: 2
    quality?: number       // JPEG/WebP quality 1-100. Default: 90
  }

### DiagramkitConfig
  interface DiagramkitConfig {
    outputDir: string          // Output folder name. Default: '.diagrams'
    manifestFile: string       // Manifest filename. Default: 'diagrams.manifest.json'
    useManifest: boolean       // Enable incremental builds. Default: true
    sameFolder: boolean        // Place outputs next to source. Default: false
    defaultFormat: OutputFormat // Default output format. Default: 'svg'
    defaultTheme: Theme        // Default theme. Default: 'both'
    extensionMap?: Record<string, DiagramType>  // Custom extension overrides
  }

### Manifest (internal)
  type Manifest = { version: 1, diagrams: Record<string, ManifestEntry> }
  type ManifestEntry = { hash: string, generatedAt: string, outputs: string[], format?: OutputFormat, theme?: Theme }

## Architecture

### Browser Pool
Single Chromium instance managed by BrowserPool (src/pool.ts):
- Lazy initialization: browser launches on first acquire()
- Reference counting: acquire() increments, release() decrements
- Idle timeout: 5 seconds after last release, browser closes automatically
- Coalesced launches: concurrent acquire() calls share one launch promise
- 4 dedicated pages: mermaid-light, mermaid-dark, excalidraw, drawio
- Mermaid needs separate light/dark pages because mermaid.initialize() is global
- Excalidraw and drawio handle dark mode per-render call
- Process cleanup on SIGINT/SIGTERM/exit

### Manifest System
SHA-256 content hashing for incremental rebuilds (src/manifest.ts):
- Each .diagrams/ folder has a diagrams.manifest.json
- Entries map source filename to hash, timestamp, output filenames, and format
- isStale() checks: hash mismatch, format change, or missing output files
- cleanOrphans() removes outputs for deleted source files
- Manifest disabled when config.useManifest is false

### Extension Aliases
Defined in src/extensions.ts. Longest-match-first resolution:
  .mermaid, .mmd, .mmdc       -> mermaid
  .excalidraw                  -> excalidraw
  .drawio, .drawio.xml, .dio  -> drawio
Custom overrides via config.extensionMap (merged with built-in map).

### Configuration
Loaded by src/config.ts with merge order:
  1. Built-in defaults (getDefaultConfig)
  2. Global config: ~/.config/diagramkit/config.json (or $XDG_CONFIG_HOME)
  3. Local config: .diagramkitrc.json (walks up directory tree)
  4. Programmatic overrides

### Dark Mode Contrast
postProcessDarkSvg() in src/color/contrast.ts:
- Scans inline style="fill:#hex" attributes in SVG
- Computes WCAG 2.0 relative luminance for each fill color
- Colors with luminance > 0.4 are darkened to lightness 0.25
- Hue and saturation (capped at 0.6) are preserved
- Ensures readable contrast against dark backgrounds
- Applied automatically to mermaid dark renders (drawio handles dark mode in-browser)

### Output Convention
Rendered images placed in .diagrams/ hidden folder next to source files:
  source.mermaid -> .diagrams/source-light.svg + .diagrams/source-dark.svg
Configurable via config.outputDir or config.sameFolder.

### Watch Mode
Uses chokidar (src/watch.ts) to watch for all supported diagram file changes (.mermaid, .mmd, .mmdc, .excalidraw, .drawio, .drawio.xml, .dio).
On change/add: re-renders affected file, updates manifest, calls onChange callback.
Returns cleanup function to stop watching.

### Renderers
Three diagram types rendered through the unified render() function in renderer.ts:
- Mermaid: uses BrowserPool mermaid pages (separate light/dark) with Playwright evaluate()
- Excalidraw: uses rolldown-bundled IIFE loaded into BrowserPool page
- Draw.io: uses rolldown-bundled IIFE that parses mxGraphModel XML to SVG
All output uses atomic writes (.tmp + rename).
Individual file failures do not abort a batch render.

## Claude Code Skills

### diagramkit (skills/claude-code/diagramkit.md)
Skill for AI-assisted diagram generation and rendering. Covers:
- CLI quick reference (render, warmup, watch, force)
- Output convention (.diagrams/ folder structure)
- Markdown linking with <picture> element for light/dark switching
- Mermaid authoring best practices (semantic IDs, node limits, neutral colors)
- Excalidraw authoring best practices (standard JSON format, spacing)
- SVG-first format guidance, with raster reserved for email/Confluence targets
- Dark mode contrast behavior explanation

## Testing

- Framework: vitest
- Unit tests: colocated with source as src/*.test.ts (manifest, color, convert, extensions, config, discovery, output)
- E2E tests: e2e/api-render.e2e.test.ts, e2e/cli-render.e2e.test.ts
- E2E helpers: e2e/test-utils.ts
- Run: npm test (all), npm run test:unit (unit only), npm run test:e2e (e2e only)
- Type checking: npm run typecheck (tsc --noEmit)
- Build: npm run build
